question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on when the audit and evaluation of the NI Primary Eyecare Assessment and Referral Service Plus Pilot will be published.
answer_text: The Northern Ireland Primary Eyecare Assessment and Referral Service Plus (NI PEARS Plus) pilot commenced in 2023/24 and ran for thirty-three weeks, with 834 patients accessing the community optometry-based service with acute eye conditions which would otherwise have presented at Belfast HSC Trust Eye Casualty. NI PEARS Plus seeks to build on the successful NI PEARS scheme which currently manages ca. 4,000 patients in community optometry settings, closer to home.As per commissioning specification, the pilot is currently being evaluated for impact, assessing metrics around activity, performance, clinical safety, patient experience and cost-effectiveness.Following evaluation, and in consultation with stakeholders, Strategic Planning and Performance Group (SPPG) will make a commissioning decision on whether the service should be mainstreamed and introduced regionally, subject to available funding.This evaluation report is anticipated mid-March 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail (i) how much was allocated to the Pathway Fund from his Department for (a) 2022-23; and (b) 2023-24; and (ii) what funding he will make available for the Pathway Fund in 2024-25.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any emergency measures he intends to put in place to support community pharmacies.
answer_text: The opening funding position for community pharmacies in 2023/24 was £128m. Additional funding has been made available in-year and with the recent allocation of funding for public service wage increases an additional £6.1m will be made available.The services provided by community pharmacy are very much appreciated and the investment into these services compares favourable with other parts of the UK.An urgent meeting was arranged this week to meet with representatives of the community pharmacy sector, CPNI to consider these matters further.  I have asked my Department to address some of the pressures that pharmacies are facing within what is available and affordable. I encouraged CPNI to continue to work with Department officials to seek ways to ensure the pharmacy service can develop in line with our shared strategic objectives.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) when the programme of drug tariff reform will be completed; and (ii) when a report will be published.
answer_text: My Department undertook a consultation seeking to improve the Drug Tariff arrangements in April 2022 and work is currently in train to take forward the first element of reform involving discount clawbacks.In light of representations from the sector, I held a meeting with representatives of CPNI and I have asked that further work is undertaken to review financial arrangements which includes elements of the Drug Tariff.At this stage, I am unable to give exact timeframes as to when this work will be completed.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the provision of Western Urgent Care out of hours service in Strabane, to detail (i) the hours it is accessible to the public; and (ii) the number of staff allocated to it.
answer_text: The Department of Health (DoH) commissions Western Urgent Care (WUC) to provide care to the public living within the Strabane area in the most efficient and appropriate means suitable for their needs.  The GP Out of Hours service (GP OOH) is available from 6pm to 8am the next morning, as well as 24 hours on weekends and during Public Holidays.  Patients or their carers contact Western Urgent Care via a single telephone number; there are no telephone numbers available to contact individual bases directly. From Apr-Dec 2023, 71% of WUC’s OOHs patient contacts were given advice by telephone or video call; 25% of patient contacts required a base visit; and 4% of contacts required a home visit.  The majority of patients who contact WUC OOHs service do not require a face-to-face appointment.Strabane baseStrabane GP OOHs base is currently open between 8am and 4pm on weekends and bank holidays with one Clinician (a GP or an Advanced Nurse Practitioner (ANP) working in the Strabane base at a time. WUC have advised DoH that this is in response to low numbers of clinicians booking shifts in the Strabane base and has been necessary in order to efficiently utilise the available resources.It is important to note that the service specification between the Department of Health and Western Urgent Care defines response targets and health related outcomes but does not specify numbers or types of staff required to deliver those outcomes. Consequently, DoH does not allocate staff to WUC nor hold numbers of same. The majority of clinical staff are retained by WUC on an ‘as and when required’ basis and dependent on the resources made available to the organisation.  DoH does encourage the development of a multidisciplinary workforce in the interests of cost efficiency.Base and Home visits for Strabane population:  If there is a request for a home visit and there is no GP in the Strabane base, a GP from another base will make the visit to wherever the patient is located. If there is no GP in the Strabane base, and a patient needs to be seen by a GP they are given the option of attending another base or waiting to be seen at their local base. When a GP from another base (e.g. Derry or Omagh) travels to Strabane, they will undertake a number of patient appointments as this is offers the most effective use of the available resources. Once these patients have been assessed and treated the GP will return to their own base or undertake any further home visits which are required. There is consistently low availability of GP’s who choose to work in OOHs at the Strabane base, therefore this arrangement allows WUC to manage patient demand and make most effective use of the available clinical resource.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance for her assessment of the relationship between fiscal devolution and long-term budget sustainability.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when the design phase for the Drumahoe Flood Alleviation Scheme will be completed.
answer_text: Development of potential flood alleviation proposals for Drumahoe remains ongoing. An economically viable scheme has been identified and consultants have been instructed to commence the design phase of this project. It is currently estimated that the design process will be completed in late 2025

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when the further review of Eglinton Flood Alleviation Scheme proposals will be completed.
answer_text: Development of flood alleviation proposals for Eglinton are currently being progressed to determine if an economically viable solution can be found.  The flooding mechanisms in Eglinton are complex with many compounding factors.  A potential solution that would mitigate the risk of flooding from the river has been identified, however, surface water flooding would still affect properties.   Therefore, my Department has appointed a consultant to take forward a wider-ranging review to explore potential opportunities to develop more holistic proposals which could provide an acceptable level of protection to homes and businesses in the area from the various sources of flooding. It is hoped this review will be completed by Spring 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on improving the zebra crossing on Main Street, Eglinton.
answer_text: Following the reported incident in December 2023, and after the PSNI investigation into the matter is concluded, officials in my department will engage with the PSNI to consider the requirement for any further safety improvements at the crossing.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on plans to improve road safety at the junction of Tamnaherin Road and Brockagh Road, Derry.
answer_text: I can confirm that the Western Division Network Development team have commissioned a feasibility study which will examine road realignment options to improve this busy junction on the Tamnaherin Road.  The project has been included on the Divisions schedule for large minor works.A detailed junction turning count took place in November 2023 and this will be used to help inform the detailed design.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on progressing the Anti-Poverty Strategy.
answer_text: I am committed to delivering long-term, sustainable solutions to poverty in all its forms for our communities right across Northern Ireland.I am currently considering the next steps to be taken in relation to an Executive Anti-Poverty Strategy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for his assessment of road safety at Ballyarnett roundabout, Derry.
answer_text: A number of safety improvements have been identified as a result of a road safety assessment, undertaken by officials in my department, at Ballyarnett Roundabout. Additional road markings will be provided to include lane directional signs to help traffic at the roundabout approach; advance lane direction signs and an expanded ‘yellow box’ marking to help prevent the impeding of through traffic on the roundabout. This work will be completed in the coming months.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities what funding is available for Neighbourhood Renewal Capital Projects in the 2024-25 financial year.
answer_text: Capital budgets have not been set for Departments for the 2024/25 financial year.  Discussions on what will be a challenging budget position are ongoing and funding for specific programmes such as Neighbourhood Renewal will become clearer when departmental allocations are finalised.The Department recognises the role that the voluntary and community sector plays in delivering critical services, support and advice to local communities and is committed to providing funding to the sector.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the new school building for St Brigid’s College, Derry.
answer_text: St Brigid’s College was announced under the Major Capital Works Programme in March 2022.  All projects which featured in this announcement were subsequently paused in April 2023 due to the current capital budget position.  In my Statement to the Assembly on 12 February I advised that seven of the twenty-eight projects announced in March 2022 would be restarted and would progress in planning.  St Brigid’s College was not one of the seven projects and thus this project remains paused at this time. For the twenty-one remaining projects on the March 2022 announced list I have confirmed that all of these projects will be kept under review and should additional capital funding be made available progression of these projects will be further considered.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the progress of the dualling scheme for the A2 Buncrana Road, Derry.
answer_text: Major Road schemes are an important part of the work that is delivered by my Department as we seek to reduce journey times, increase reliability and improve road safety.The A2 Buncrana Road scheme is critical to providing a key link in the North West and to County Donegal. Work on the A2 Buncrana Road scheme was paused through a decision taken under the NI (Executive Formation etc) Act 2022, to allow appropriate alignment with the emerging transport plans.The A2 Buncrana Road scheme will be considered as part of the North West Transport Plan  which will be subject to a public consultation process.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) how many patients benefited from the Cross-Border Healthcare Scheme, broken down by Health and Social Care Trust; and (ii) what consideration he has given to reintroduce the Cross-Border Healthcare Scheme.
answer_text: Following the UK exit from the EU the Cross-Border Healthcare Directive (CBHD) no longer applies in the UK. The provisions for the CBHD in NI domestic legislation were revoked and there was an orderly closedown of the scheme with remaining reimbursements being processed by the Health and Social Care Board (HSCB) in 2021/22.As you will be aware, I introduced the Republic of Ireland Reimbursement Scheme (RoIRS) on 30 June 2021 as part of the Elective Care Framework to help reduce NI waiting lists. The scheme was a limited 12-month administrative version of the Cross-Border Healthcare Directive (CBHD) which ceased to apply to NI following the UK’s exit from the EU. In June 2022, I announced that the RoIRS would be extended beyond the planned 30 June 2022 deadline, until an additional £5m of new funding had been committed. The RoIRS closed to new applications on Wednesday 21 September 2022 when the additional £5m funding had been exhausted. In response to your enquiry the number of patients that benefited from the RoIRS scheme broken down by Health and Social Care Trust is as follows –Western Health and Social Care Trust – 708Southern Health and Social Care Trust – 889South Eastern Health and Social Care Trust – 561Northern Health and Social Care Trust – 771Belfast Health and Social Care Trust - 346 Any future funding allocation to a similar scheme to help reduce waiting lists will now need to be considered as part of a broader review of resource investment in Waiting List Initiatives.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for a breakdown of the £5.3 million support package planned for Community Pharmacies.
answer_text: 1.	Community pharmacy representatives have highlighted inflationary pressures in relation to the cost of medicines. In the last number of months there has been a marked increase in the market prices of a small number of medicines versus the reference Drug Tariff price. Where this is identified, community pharmacies are reimbursed at the higher price and this is known as a concessionary price.2.	While relatively small in number, the cost of concessionary prices has risen each month and at September was £1.7m.3.	This additional cost is fully reimbursed. However, as community pharmacies are having to manage the risk in advance of concessionary prices being awarded, my Department has increased the amount of concessionary price advance by £0.5m to £1.5m and will keep this under review.4.	Furthermore, my Department had provided a special advance payment of £35m to community pharmacies at the beginning of the COVID-19 emergency.  This has been reclaimed on a gradual basis be retracting £1.4m each month. Given the cashflow issues being reported, my Department has suspended this retraction for a period of three months.5.	Finally, my Department is processing additional funding of £0.6m to community pharmacy from existing programme funds. It is anticipated these measure will alleviate the issues being faced and my Department will keep this issue under review.Collectively the three measures detailed above provide funding for the £5.3 million support package planned for Community Pharmacies

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to list the eleven dental practices taking part in the pilot scheme to improve access to urgent dental care for non-registered patients, including refugees and asylum seekers, by Trust area.
answer_text: Unfortunately due to there being no Minister in post after midnight on the 27th of October, my Department will be unable to answer the question.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) when his programme of Drug Tariff reform (a) commenced; and (b) will be completed; and (ii) when a report will be published.
answer_text: The Department of Health and Social Care (DHSC) in England consulted on a number of community pharmacy drug reimbursement reform proposals back in 2019. DHSC are now beginning to implement these changes to the English Drug Tariff, and as the Northern Ireland Drug Tariff (NIDT) is currently reflective of the English Drug Tariff, a consultation on potential changes to the Northern Ireland Tariff was issued by the Department of Health in March 2022.My Department will be working together with our colleagues in Community Pharmacy Northern Ireland (CPNI) on the outworkings of this consultation over the coming months, including consideration of potential impacts and mitigations.There is no fixed completion date yet as this will be determined following the ongoing consultation with stakeholders which include CPNI.The Northern Ireland Drug Tariff is published on a monthly basis and published on the BSO website. Drug Tariff (hscni.net). All agreed changes in relation to the NIDT will be published on the BSO website.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what commitment his Department has made to help complete or agree the business case for the Northlands Addiction Centre in Foyle.
answer_text: This question may relate to the commitment under Annex A to the “New Decade, New Approach” (NDNA) document, in the context of the agreement to restore the NI Executive, when the UK Government outlined Financial and Economic Commitments to Northern Ireland. Under the section Addressing Northern Ireland’s Unique Circumstances, the UK Government stated that it would make funding available for a range of projects aimed at supporting community and reconciliation initiatives to remove barriers, bring the people together and build a safer, more secure society, which could include areas such as additional funding to support the Derry/Londonderry addiction centre.Following discussions with the Northern Ireland Office (NIO), my officials, along with the Public Health Agency (PHA) and the Strategic Planning & Performance Group (SPPG) as the commissioners of substance use services, have been looking at how best to develop potential proposals for the allocation of funding under this commitment.As an initial step, a population needs-based assessment of substance use and related services in the Western Health & Social Care Trust area is now underway and a review of inpatient Tier 4 services across Northern Ireland is also being commissioned.In addition, my Department is leading on the implementation of Preventing Harm, Empowering Recovery, NI’s new substance use strategy. As part of this, SPPG in association with PHA are in the process of developing an approach to Multi-Agency Strategic Planning and Commissioning. It is the intention that a comprehensive, whole-system, planning approach to substance use will ensure that demographic trends of increasing demand and complexity, current and future resource, gaps, service pressures and workforce issues will be addressed.All of these pieces of work will be utilised so that my Department can make informed proposals to the NIO around this NDNA commitment.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether (i) consideration was given to increasing the non means-tested student nurse bursary of £5,165 per annum; and (ii) there are plans to review the rate, which remains at 2012/13 levels.
answer_text: The effectiveness of my Department’s support arrangements for nursing and midwifery students is kept under regular review.  While I announced on 27 September 2022 changes to the rates of specific means-tested allowances to alleviate the impact of the current cost of living pressures, affordability is an important factor in considering any further changes to the wider range of bursary support.  Under the current proposed budget allocations, my Department is facing a very significant funding shortfall, and difficult decisions would be required before I could commit any additional funding in this area.  In addition, the very high ratio of applicants to the nursing pre-registration programmes and the low rates of attrition, indicates that the current arrangements continue to be effective.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the outcomes of the joint workshop, which was held with Minister for Infrastructure, on seeking solutions to constraints on housing supply issues, particularly in areas of high housing need.
answer_text: My draft Housing Supply Strategy recognises a number of interconnected barriers to delivery of the supply we need. The workshop jointly hosted by my Department and the Department for Infrastructure was held specifically to examine the issue of wastewater infrastructure. A range of key stakeholders and delivery partners attended the event and discussion was wide ranging, focusing on both potential strategic and tactical solutions. My officials are currently preparing a report for review by Minister O’Dowd and myself on the outcome of these discussions and to detail thinking on potential actions to be taken forward.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when a timeline for the rollout of the HPV primary screening will be published.
answer_text: The Cancer Strategy, published in March 2022, included the action that HPV testing will be introduced into Northern Ireland’s Cervical Screening Programme and it was envisaged that project work would begin in 2022/23. This involves significant work to reconfigure laboratory services and increase assessment and treatment capacity in colposcopy services and my Department is currently working with the Public Health Agency (PHA) and Regional Pathology Network to progress this action.  It is anticipated that this will be partially completed within the next 12 months but is dependent on confirmation of budgets to support this service change.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what plans are in place to ensure that the changes to the cervical screening programme are communicated clearly and effectively to the public.
answer_text: The Public Health Agency (PHA) will develop a comprehensive communication strategy in advance of the planned changes to the screening programme. This will include informing women of the changes to how screening tests will be processed and what the results mean. The communication strategy will take into account learning from other UK nations where changes have already been put in place.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health will the planned rollout of HPV primary screening include the opportunity for the quick adoption of HPV self-sampling, as recommended by the UK National Screening Committee.
answer_text: HPV self-sampling is not currently recommended by the UK National Screening Committee (UK NSC). A pilot research study for self-sampling called ‘Youscreen,’ specifically targeting areas in London where participation in cervical screening has traditionally been low, took place in 2021. Once the results from the study are published, the findings will be considered by the UK NSC. However, self-sampling for HPV will not replace a test carried out by a health professional; it is a pre-test to indicate who may need a test carried out by a health professional.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the Phase 2 delivery of the Health and Social Care Staff COVID Recognition Payment, including (i) the total monies paid out under Phase 2; (ii) the number of people who have received the payment; and (iii) when all eligible workers will receive this payment.
answer_text: Phase 2 had been temporarily paused to allow time to seek clarity on a number of issues raised relating to the impact of the increase in national insurance contributions which are included as part of the payment provided to the former employer.  A further letter was issued to employers on 14 April 2022 providing clarity on the issues raised and confirming recommencement of the payment process. Claims are being processed by BSO in date order when all relevant information is provided by the claimants. No date has been set for the closure of the scheme and claims will continue to be processed by the Business Services Organisation during 2022/23. I can confirm that as at 29th June 2022, a total of over £4.49m has been paid out to 10,399 individuals under Phase 2. This figures include staff previously employed in independent sector care home and domiciliary care providers who have since moved employer or left the sector, and staff employed with eligible day opportunity providers and buildings-based day services.Additionally, the Scheme is now open to eligible applicants in the following groups, Personal Assistants employed by Independent Living Fund recipients, Thalidomide Health Grant recipients, and Personal Assistants employed by those in receipt of Direct Payments from HSC Trusts. This element of the scheme was launched on 23 March and is being delivered by ILF Scotland for the payments, Scotland Excel who set up and run the online portal, and SDS Scotland who manage the dedicated helpline. These delivery partners were heavily involved in delivering the equivalent scheme for the Scottish Government.  To date almost £561k has been paid to 1,775 individuals. To encourage uptake on this element Trusts have now written to all individuals in receipt of direct payments. I can confirm that on the 17th June 2022 my officials launched an online portal which will allow those in the independent sector whose employers have ceased trading to apply for the Special Recognition Payment.Staff who worked in the independent sector (care home/domiciliary care/supported living) whose employer is no longer trading are eligible to apply for this payment if they were employed for a continuous 4 week period between 17 March 2020 and 31 January 2021. Applications can be made online and a dedicated helpline is also available from 10am – 12.30pm Monday to Friday. A FAQ on the Special Recognition Payment has been published on the Department’s website: - https://www.health-ni.gov.uk/news/hsc-staff-recognition-payment-faqsThis continues to be updated with relevant information as each phase of the claims process progresses. I remain personally committed to ensuring the recognition payment continues to be paid to all eligible independent sector staff as quickly as possible.
